root_text.sn45.10:1.2: Atha kho nandiyo paribbājako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn45.10:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho nandiyo paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn45.10:1.4: “kati nu kho, bho gotama, dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā nibbānaṅgamā honti nibbānaparāyanā nibbānapariyosānā”ti?
root_text.sn45.10:2.1: “Aṭṭhime kho, nandiya, dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā nibbānaṅgamā honti nibbānaparāyanā nibbānapariyosānā.
root_text.sn45.10:2.4: Ime kho, nandiya, aṭṭha dhammā bhāvitā bahulīkatā nibbānaṅgamā honti nibbānaparāyanā nibbānapariyosānā”ti.
root_text.sn45.10:2.5: Evaṁ vutte, nandiyo paribbājako bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn45.10:2.7: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhavaṁ gotamo dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn45.10:1.2: Then the wanderer Nandiya went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn45.10:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side, and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.sn45.10:1.4: “Mister Gotama, how many things, when developed and cultivated, have extinguishment as their target, destination, and culmination?”
translation_text.sn45.10:2.1: “These eight things, when developed and cultivated, have extinguishment as their target, destination, and culmination.
translation_text.sn45.10:2.3: They are: right view, right thought, right speech, right action, right livelihood, right effort, right mindfulness, and right immersion.
translation_text.sn45.10:2.4: These eight things, when developed and cultivated, have extinguishment as their target, destination, and culmination.”
translation_text.sn45.10:2.5: When he said this, the wanderer Nandiya said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn45.10:2.7: From this day forth, may the worthy Gotama remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”
